title: Day 06 — Custom Hooks and Responsive Layout
summary: Extract debounce and theme logic into typed custom hooks, add debounced search, theme toggle, and responsive sidebar/navbar composition.

blocks.content: Your dashboard routes work. Now make it feel like a product: search that does not spam APIs, dark mode, sidebar that survives mobile. Custom hooks are how teams share logic without copy-paste — useDebounce in search, useTheme in navbar, useMediaQuery later. Tomorrow you rebuild all of Week 1 in 90 minutes. Today you sharpen the architecture.

blocks.title: Scope for Day 06
blocks.content: Custom hooks, debounce, responsive layout, theme toggle. You may combine with Day 04 fetch and Day 05 routes. Context API and Redux come in Week 2–3.
Without debounce, every keystroke in search triggers a filter or fetch. Debounce waits until typing pauses — typically 300ms — then runs once. Implementation uses setTimeout and clearTimeout.

blocks.text: React — Custom hooks and layout composition

blocks.content: When two components need the same stateful logic, extract a custom hook. Navbar uses useTheme. SearchBar uses useDebounce. Layout components compose Sidebar and Navbar without duplicating mobile toggle code.
Desktop: sidebar fixed left. Mobile: sidebar hidden until hamburger opens drawer overlay. Use CSS media queries and isSidebarOpen state. Navbar holds hamburger, search, theme toggle.

items.text: Sidebar slides or overlays on mobile
Backdrop or close button dismisses drawer
Theme toggle reachable on mobile navbar

blocks.text: Build Mission — Hooks and responsive dashboard

blocks.content: Debounce stuck? Check [value, delay] deps. Theme flash? Read localStorage in effect after mount.
Same setTimeout in two files? Extract useDebounce once in src/hooks/.

blocks.title: Tomorrow: Machine Coding Round 1
blocks.content: 90 minutes. No AI. No Google. No copy-paste from old code. Build login + dashboard + sidebar + navbar from memory. Review today's hooks and layout patterns tonight.
